summary.intent: Test the `reflect init` flow from a completely clean state, verifying that qmd embedding works correctly with CPU-only llama.cpp prebuilt binaries and that the new broader triage/dynamic categories/auto qmd reindex logic in `reflect ingest` functions properly.
summary.outcome: The `reflect init` flow was successfully validated end-to-end (qmd collection registration, skill install, empty-wiki embed skip). The `reflect ingest` test was aborted mid-run due to slowness; the session ended with committing skill re-syncs and a `.gitignore` update.

repo: node-llama-cpp auto-selects the Vulkan prebuilt over the CPU-only prebuilt when both are installed, then fails to compile from source if the Vulkan SDK is absent — Vulkan package must be explicitly removed to force CPU path.
`reflect init` correctly skips the seed embed when the wiki is empty (no pages to index), confirming the empty-wiki guard works.
The qmd skill is installed to `.claude/skills/qmd/` as a dedicated artifact separate from the `reflect` skill.
Stale qmd collections must be explicitly removed after wiping `.reflect/` state, otherwise they retain broken path references.

code.finding: Modified as part of skill re-sync commit.
Modified as part of skill re-sync commit.
Updated to exclude transient state (likely `.reflect/wiki.bak` or similar) so the working tree stays clean during test cycles.

workflow: Piping long-running CLI output through `tail -30` silently buffers everything until completion, making progress invisible — run commands directly or use a log file for live output.
When testing from zero, both the `.reflect/` directory and the qmd collection registration must be wiped independently.
Background ingest processes can stall indefinitely on evidence-gathering steps when the CLI is slow; kill-and-retry is more reliable than waiting.
The stop-hook git check (`~/.claude/stop-hook-git-check.sh`) fires mid-test and requires uncommitted changes to be resolved before the session can cleanly end.

friction: llama.cpp compiled from source for ~11 minutes before the missing prebuilt package was identified as the root cause.
The Vulkan prebuilt was silently preferred over the CPU prebuilt, causing a confusing compile failure with no Vulkan SDK installed.
Piping embed output through `tail` masked all progress, making it appear the process was hung when it was actually running.
A bash wrapper process remained hung after the underlying node process exited, requiring a separate kill step.
`reflect ingest` stalled on evidence gathering, making the ingest test inconclusive.
The stop-hook git check interrupted the testing workflow and forced a context switch to commit housekeeping changes.
open_items: The `reflect ingest --verbose` test with the new broader triage, dynamic categories, and auto qmd reindex was never completed — needs a re-run in a less constrained environment.
Investigate why `reflect ingest` stalls on evidence gathering when the CLI is slow; may need a timeout or chunking strategy.
Confirm that `.gitignore` correctly excludes all transient test artifacts (e.g., `wiki.bak`) so future test cycles don't leave dirty working trees.
